text: Forests talk — through a hidden fungal internet under your feet. This isn’t magic; it’s mycelium, a network of fungi connecting tree roots. Trees share nutrients and even send distress signals when they’re under threat. Studies show that trees can warn each other about pests through this underground network. This ‘wood-wide web’ is essential for forest survival, fostering cooperation over competition. Want to learn more about nature's secrets? Hit that follow button!
